_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that families come in many different shapes and forms, and every family is special.

_source.w: We are learning that families come in many different shapes and forms, and every family is special.
y: Understanding that families look different helps us respect and include everyone in our class.
Learning about different family types helps us see that love and care are what make a family, not what it looks like.
Knowing about diverse families connects us to the real world, where communities are made up of all kinds of people.
eg: We might share about the people in our family — like parents, grandparents, aunties, uncles, or foster carers.
We could read stories about children who live in different types of families and talk about what's the same and different.
We might draw our family and compare it with a friend's, noticing that every family is unique.
We could talk about how in some cultures, like Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities, family includes a wide network of kin.
We might discuss how some children live with one parent, two parents, grandparents, or other family members.
We could make a class display celebrating all the different families in our room.
